commit.message:
fix(makefile): resolve pg_isready path on apple silicon and intel macs (#29)

detect postgresql@16 bin dir at make-eval time via ls, supporting both
/opt/homebrew (apple silicon) and /usr/local (intel) homebrew prefixes,
and use the full path in pg_isready/psql/createdb recipe commands so
/bin/sh invoked by make always finds the tools regardless of shell PATH

Add transient error retry with exponential backoff (#109) (#220)

* Add transient error retry with exponential backoff to LLMExecutor (#109)

Centralize retry logic for transient errors (rate limits, timeouts,
connection failures) in LLMExecutor with configurable exponential
backoff. Permanent errors fall through immediately to the next model.

- Add RetryConfig dataclass with max_retries, base_delay, max_delay,
  backoff_multiplier
- Add is_transient_error() classifier using pattern matching
- Extend LLMExecutor.run() with per-model retry loop
- Wire default RetryConfig in PlanTask.create_llm_executor()
- Simplify deduplicate_levers.py retry flow (transient retries now
  handled by executor; compact-history fallback retained)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Revert deduplicate_levers.py changes from retry PR

Restore the original two-stage retry flow (full history, then
compacted history) instead of nesting the second attempt inside
the first except block.

commit.message:
feat: learning system — implicit feedback, memory extraction, session replay, analytics

Wire real quality signals into the routing fitness cache via implicit feedback
emitted after each CLI session. Add memory auto-extraction from session
resolution patterns, session replay APIs, and analytics endpoints with
dashboard panels.

Phase 1 - Fitness from real usage:
- CLI emits implicit quality feedback based on stop reason + branch detection
- Traffic refresh merges feedback quality via Bayesian blending (prior_weight=5)
- ClickHouse materialized view for hourly quality aggregation

Phase 2 - Memory auto-extraction:
- Heuristic extraction: next-steps, file co-modification patterns, resolutions
- Memory access tracking (access_count, last_accessed_at) in uglyhat

Phase 3 - Session replay:
- GET /api/v1/sessions — list sessions grouped by episode_id
- GET /api/v1/sessions/:episode_id — per-turn detail with feedback

Phase 4 - Analytics:
- GET /api/v1/analytics/quality-trends
- GET /api/v1/analytics/routing-savings (counterfactual via MODEL_CATALOG)
- GET /api/v1/analytics/session-efficiency
- Dashboard panels for all three analytics sections

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix blank screen caused by duplicate React instances in npm workspaces

The workspace setup installed separate react copies in each package's
node_modules, so shared code (AuthContext) used a different React instance
than the apps. Hooks break silently with multiple React instances. Moved
react/react-dom to peerDependencies in shared package and added Vite
resolve.dedupe to force a single instance.

  currentColor que se perdia contra el fondo claro

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: handle DataType::Null in adjust_child_validity to prevent panic (#6160)

Previously, `adjust_child_validity` would call `ArrayData::try_new` with
a null bitmap on a `DataType::Null` array, causing an `.unwrap()` panic
with `InvalidArgumentError("Arrays of type Null cannot contain a null
bitmask")`.

The trigger: when a user inserts rows where a struct sub-field has only
null values, Arrow infers `DataType::Null` for that column. If a
subsequent fragment omits that nullable sub-field, Lance inserts a
`NullReader` to fill it in. `MergeStream` then merges the real batch
(with null struct rows) and the `NullReader` batch (all-null struct),
recursing into the struct where `adjust_child_validity` is called with
the `Null`-typed child and a non-empty parent validity — triggering the
panic.

Fix: skip the bitmask operation when `child.data_type() ==
DataType::Null`. A `Null` array is always entirely null by definition
and needs no validity adjustment.

Closes https://github.com/lance-format/lance/issues/6159

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix setup.ps1 encoding: add UTF-8 BOM for PowerShell 5.1

PowerShell 5.1 on Windows defaults to system ANSI codepage (GBK on
Chinese systems), causing Chinese characters to be garbled and parsed
as syntax errors. UTF-8 BOM makes PS 5.1 read the file correctly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: robust column width preservation across all save/load paths

Root cause: three bugs caused column widths to corrupt on save:
1. XML attribute regex assumed fixed order (Id before Target),
   but attribute order in XML is not guaranteed
2. Encrypted saves: xlsx-populate's final outputAsync re-corrupted
   column widths after fixExcelJSColWidths had fixed them
3. Encrypted file reopen: decryptIfNeeded didn't fix column widths,
   so savedOriginalColsXml captured already-corrupted data

Fixes:
- Add buildSheetNameMapping() utility with order-independent XML parsing
- Add restoreColWidthsInXlsxPopulate() to set correct widths in
  xlsx-populate's model before outputAsync (encryption step)
- Add captureColWidthsFromXlsxPopulate() to capture widths from
  xlsx-populate model before outputAsync corrupts them
- Fix decryptIfNeeded to restore column widths after decryption
- Refactor fixExcelJSColWidths to use buildSheetNameMapping

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(backend): ✨ implement MIR→LLVM IR backend with type lowering (#87)

## Summary

Implements the initial LLVM backend (Task 11), completing the compiler
pipeline from Dao source through MIR to LLVM IR. Also enforces explicit
`return` as a language rule, adds bare `return` support for void
functions, and hardens the backend against silent miscompilation.

## Highlights

- **LlvmTypeLowering**: all builtins, void, pointer, string as `{i8*,
i64}`, struct layout; explicit failure for enums/generics
- **LlvmBackend**: full instruction lowering — constants, unary/binary
ops (signed/unsigned/float), store/load, FnRef, call, Br/CondBr/Return
- **Explicit failure contract**: projected places (field/index/deref),
`mode parallel`/`gpu`, resource regions, lambda, iteration all rejected
with diagnostics — no silent miscompilation
- **Backend API safety**: module nulled on error; callers cannot consume
partial IR
- **`daoc llvm-ir` command**: fail-on-error exit semantics, no partial
IR on stdout
- **Explicit return enforced**: `CONTRACT_SYNTAX_SURFACE.md` updated —
no implicit tail-expression return; parser supports bare `return` for
void functions
- **Conan migration**: `conanfile.txt` → `conanfile.py` for LLVM option
configuration (X86-only, reduced deps)
- **24 backend tests**: type lowering, functions, arithmetic, control
flow, strings, calls, module structure, plus negative tests for
projected places and mode/resource rejection

## Test plan

- [x] `ctest --test-dir build/debug` — 10/10 pass (including 24 new
backend assertions)
- [x] `daoc llvm-ir` exits 0 on valid input, exits 1 with diagnostic on
unsupported constructs
- [x] Projected places (`p.y = 1`, `&p.y`) rejected with diagnostic
- [x] `mode parallel =>` and `resource memory =>` rejected; `mode unsafe
=>` accepted as no-op
- [x] All examples/probes updated to explicit `return`, golden ASTs
regenerated

## Breaking Changes

- `conanfile.txt` removed, replaced by `conanfile.py` — re-run `conan
install`
- Dao now requires explicit `return` in all block-bodied functions;
implicit tail-expression return is no longer accepted

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: strip \?\ extended path prefix from bundled engine paths

Tauri's resource_dir() returns paths with the \?\ extended-length
prefix on Windows. PowerShell 5.1's Split-Path cannot parse drive
letters from these paths, causing $script:EndstateRoot to be null
and breaking all bundled engine commands.

Strip the prefix in both code paths: get_bundled_engine_path (non-
streaming exec for capabilities) and build_bundled_engine_command
(streaming exec via engine_adapter).

Bumps version to 1.1.1.

commit.message:
fix(runner): project_bank resolution, TaskCreate tools, signal dedup

Three fixes to pipeline_runner.py:

1. CLAUDE_PROJECT_BANK now derived from target_dir (the project being
   worked on) instead of repo root (the harness). Supports explicit
   `project_bank` DOT graph attribute for override. Fixes workers using
   wrong Hindsight bank when runner lives in harness but edits cross-repo.

2. Replace legacy TodoWrite with TaskCreate/TaskUpdate/TaskList/TaskGet
   in _BASE_TOOLS. These are the current task tracking tools with richer
   features (dependencies, owners, status spinners).

3. Fix double validation dispatch and timeout-overwrites-pass bugs:
   - SDK completion handler now checks node status before writing signal
     (skips if node already past 'active' — worker wrote signal in-flight)
   - Validation timeout handler checks node status before writing fail
     (skips if node already 'validated' — validation passed via Write tool
     but SDK call hadn't completed before 600s timeout)

Root cause evidence: Logfire trace 019cda11adf835f209e0a768a478001a
showed voice_agent_model getting pass→pass→fail sequence because
timeout handler overwrote validation pass after 600s.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add jwt-p8 auth type for Apple APIs

Implements ES256 JWT generation from a PKCS8 EC private key (.p8 file),
enabling keychain-backed authentication against App Store Connect, APNs,
and Sign in with Apple without manual token rotation.

- New `jwt-p8` auth type: profile flags --key-id, --issuer-id, --jwt-audience
- `authbear login --key-file` validates and stores .p8 key in OS keychain
- `--delete-after-store` removes the key file from disk after storage
- Fresh 20-minute JWT generated on every call/token invocation
- Full unit and integration test coverage including signature verification
- Docs updated: README, CHANGELOG, USAGE
